We had a lovely lunch on a warm San Francisco day on the Embarcadero, yet it seemed like we were dining in Paris.  We were a group of 6 with two dogs, so we dined outside on a beautiful day.  The staff was attentive and welcoming.  We were celebrating an anniversary and the staff made us feel very special.  The food was delicious which made the experience so special.

I had lunch at Bon Délire, which is a newish French-inspired restaurant at Pier 3 along the Embarcadero (the waterfront). 

I grabbed a table outside and next to the sidewalk for a lunch with Parisian-style people watching. 

Service was slow even though the restaurant was hardly busy.

The wine list is good but glasses of wine are overpriced. $30 for champagne is excessive. 

No bread is served, which I thought a bit unusual. At any restaurant in Paris you would get a basket of bread as soon as you sat down.

My caesar salad with salmon looked good but the salmon was uncooked at the center. Almost raw. How does a kitchen not know how to properly cook salmon?

The waiter didn't apologize or offer anything as a recovery. The manager Constantino came out, comped a glass of wine, brought me a cheese and bread plate and had the chef prepare a new caesar salad. This time, the salmon was perfectly cooked. 

So, kudos to the manager for handling the situation well.

The waiter never offered dessert or even a coffee.

All things considered, I probably wouldn't return until the service can be improved. Bon Délire is not inexpensive. $30 for a salad with salmon. $16 for a glass of rose and $30 for a glass of champagne. For the prices charged, the service needs to be better. And quicker. Especially at lunch where many people only have an hour.
